description: Textbook migraine with aura in a healthy young woman, no red flags. Specificity control: the engine must NOT escalate to rare/dangerous zebra workups.

transcript.text: Once or twice a month I get a throbbing headache on one side, with nausea, and bright lights bother me. I have to lie down in the dark.
Anything happen before the headache starts?
Yes, about 20 minutes before, I see zigzag shimmering lines that drift across my vision, then they fade and the headache comes.
Any weakness, fever, or worst-headache-of-your-life onset?
No, nothing like that. I'm healthy otherwise and they've been like this for years.
